missed_disqualifying_criteria.criterion_text: Thyroid hormones within normal range without anti thyroid drugs
Recovery of pancreatitis by tolerance of oral intake (defined as 24 hours of food consumption of any consistency without emetic episodes and pain defined as 4/10 on the visual analogue score of pain)

errors.criterion_text: Thyroid hormones within normal range without anti thyroid drugs
Serology compatible with primary EBV infection (anti-EBNA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgM positive OR anti-EBNA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgG positive, anti-VCA IgM positive)
If patient: Graves' disease with positive TSH-receptor antibodies and thyroid hormones above the upper reference limit at diagnosis
Patients with known or suspected CAEBV must be 1 year of age or older
Biliary pancreatitis diagnosed on imaging (ultrasound, magnetic resonance imaging and/or tomography)
Recovery of pancreatitis by tolerance of oral intake (defined as 24 hours of food consumption of any consistency without emetic episodes and pain defined as 4/10 on the visual analogue score of pain)

analysis: Overall accuracy is 82% (33/40 labeled criteria matched exactly, n=40 total labels, 0 unmatched due to criterion-text drift). The weakest verdict class is UNCERTAIN at 20% recall, meaning the matcher most often mislabels this ground-truth category as something else. 1 case(s) where the true label is UNKNOWN (vignette genuinely silent) were instead called MET or NOT_MET -- the matcher's most consequential failure mode, since it manufactures certainty the source data doesn't support. The remaining 6 error(s) are boundary calls between UNCERTAIN and a definite verdict, which is a lower-stakes miss than the UNKNOWN-to-definite case. Labels were hand-written from criterion text + vignette alone, blind to the matcher's own output, so this number reflects genuine matcher accuracy rather than label leakage.
